methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $626M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Poolside is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Jason Warner and Eiso Kant, having raised $626M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates against a comp universe of 9 private firms and 1 public peer (PLTR), all operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer set includes Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression anchors to the $626M total raised as a proxy for implied current value, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the next funding stage, yielding $1.2B. Peer-Median benchmarks Poolside against the five named comps plus three additional private firms in the same sector and stage; the median valuation across this set is $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity in how the market prices frontier AI firms at this stage.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $1.0B (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $3.1B. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median suggests material uncertainty around whether Poolside's capital efficiency and progress track closer to earlier-stage norms or to the valuations commanded by better-capitalized peers. Key uncertainties include the strength of Poolside's differentiation within its specific AI domain and the sustainability of current market pricing for Series B frontier-tech companies.
